name: mitogen (Fast Python Remote Execution Framework)

auth.notes: SSH-based remote execution; uses system SSH configuration

performance.notes: 10-25x faster than Ansible for equivalent tasks due to persistent SSH connections and serialized execution

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance remote execution framework. Runs Python functions on remote hosts via SSH. import mitogen; import mitogen.utils. @mitogen.main() def main(router): context = router.ssh(hostname='web1'). context.call(os.path.exists, '/tmp/test'). context.call(subprocess.check_output, ['uname', '-a']). Persistent connection per host (much faster than ssh+fork per task). Ansible integration: ansible-mitogen accelerates Ansible 10-25x by replacing connection plugin. import mitogen.select for async fan-out. sudo contexts: router.sudo(). Thread-safe. Zero dependencies on remote host beyond Python 2.7+/3.x.

gotchas: Complex API — higher learning curve than Fabric
Primary use case is Ansible acceleration via ansible-mitogen plugin
Less maintained recently (2022+); some Ansible version compatibility issues
Direct use (not via Ansible) requires understanding of contexts and routers
Error handling in remote calls requires careful attention
best_when: Ansible users wanting 10-25x speedup via ansible-mitogen plugin
High-performance Python function execution on multiple remote hosts
When SSH connection overhead is a bottleneck in automation
Custom remote automation where Fabric's simplicity isn't enough
avoid_when: When ansible-mitogen compatibility with Ansible version is uncertain
Teams new to remote Python execution
summary: mitogen executes Python functions on remote hosts via persistent SSH connections — 10-25x faster than Ansible. @mitogen.main() def main(router): ctx = router.ssh(hostname='host'); ctx.call(subprocess.check_output, ['cmd']). BSD-3. Primary use: ansible-mitogen Ansible accelerator. Complex API. Best for Ansible speedup or high-perf remote Python execution. Use Fabric for simpler SSH automation.
